senses.definition: a period or instance of advertising that occurs before a main event or product launch
examples: The film trailer was played as a preadvertisement before the movie.
The campaign included a preadvertisement phase to build interest.

senses.definition: a person or entity that conducts advertising prior to a main event or product release
examples: The preadvertiser released teaser content ahead of the product launch.
Industry observers noted the preadvertiser's strategy as highly aggressive.

senses.definition: advertising that happens before a product, event, or campaign officially begins
examples: Preadvertising helped create anticipation for the new smartphone.
Critics argued that preadvertising was intrusive.

senses.definition: advice given before a decision or action; preliminary guidance
examples: The mentor offered pre advice on how to approach the project.
We sought pre advice from legal counsel before signing.

senses.definition: able to be advised beforehand or foreseen; capable of being anticipated
examples: The risks were preadvisable, so we prepared a contingency plan.
That outcome was preadvisable given the early signs.

senses.definition: to advise beforehand; to give guidance prior to an action
examples: We preadvise clients on best practices before they begin.
The consultant preadvised the team to consider alternatives.

senses.definition: advised beforehand; given guidance prior to an action
examples: The plan was preadvised by the committee.
We preadvised the participants about the risks.

senses.definition: a person who provides advice beforehand
examples: The preadviser helped outline options before the meeting.
Consultants act as preadvisers to startups.

senses.definition: the act of advising beforehand; providing early guidance
examples: Preadvising is common in high-stakes projects.
The team valued preadvising during planning sessions.

senses.definition: relating to advice given beforehand
examples: The board issued a preadvisory note to stakeholders.
Preadvisory warnings were posted on the site.

senses.definition: advocacy that occurs before a case or issue is formally presented
examples: Preadvocacy aims to shape opinions before a policy debate.
They engaged in preadvocacy to mobilize early support.

senses.definition: to advocate beforehand; to support a cause before formal debate
examples: They preadvocate the bill to gather early support.
Experts preadvocate diverse viewpoints during consultations.

senses.definition: advocated beforehand; supported a cause prior to formal debate
examples: The plan was preadvocated by several stakeholders.
He had preadvocated the policy in early meetings.

senses.definition: the act of advocating beforehand; early promotion of a cause
examples: Preadvocating can influence public opinion before debates.
The group focuses on preadvocating for environmental reform.

senses.definition: relating to or occurring before a festival
examples: The preaestival events drew crowds days ahead of the festival.
Preaestival preparations included site setup and volunteering signups.

senses.definition: an effect or feeling that precedes a reaction or action
examples: Pre affect influenced how the team interpreted the data.
There was a pre affect of anticipation in the room.

senses.definition: an early or initial feeling of liking or fondness before deeper affection develops
examples: Children often show preaffection toward pets before bonding fully.
Preaffection can grow into strong friendship over time.

senses.definition: an affidavit prepared in advance or before formal submission
examples: The lawyer filed a preaffidavit to speed up the process.
A preaffidavit was reviewed for accuracy before testimony.

senses.definition: a person or entity that becomes affiliated before a formal agreement
examples: The company recruited a preaffiliate network ahead of the merger.
Preaffiliate partners were asked to sign provisional terms.

senses.definition: affiliated beforehand or prior to formal agreement
examples: The preaffiliated groups agreed to preliminary terms.
Preaffiliated firms joined the consortium early.

senses.definition: the act of affiliating beforehand; forming relationships prior to formal agreement
examples: Several startups are preaffiliating to join the alliance.
Preaffiliating helps participants align goals early.

senses.definition: an affiliation formed before a formal agreement or joining
examples: The preaffiliation created a foundation for cooperation.
Preaffiliation terms were discussed in the early meetings.

senses.definition: to affirm or confirm in advance; to state as true beforehand
examples: The spokesperson preaffirms the policy to reassure the public.
We preaffirm the key principles at the outset.

senses.definition: an affirmation given in advance; a prior confirmation
examples: The preaffirmation set the tone for the discussion.
There was a preaffirmation of support from the committee.

senses.definition: serving to affirm in advance; relating to prior confirmation
examples: The preaffirmative stance guided the negotiations.
There was a preaffirmative mood among the participants.
